calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Payment Of Fees To Transfer File To Sacramento County
Off Calendar Apr-24-2018 Text Ruling

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-24-2018 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Payment Of Fees To Transfer File To Sacramento County
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Continued To Apr-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee and the Attorney to appear on April 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the fees to transfer file to Sacramento County has not been paid. The Court noted that today's hearing was set by the transfer order, and that parties should have been aware of it. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION TO TRANSFER TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-22-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Armon Cooper for and with Janet Welborn (Successor Trustee) appeared by telephone.

Mr. Cooper stated that the he filed the final accounting with a hearing date of March 6, 2018 and that a petition for change of venue is pending on e-file. MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Armon Cooper for Petitioner.

The Court asks Counsel how he feels about having one Trustee and informed him that it is not inclined to approve monthly payments/fees during the first account period when there is no track record to review. Mr. Cooper stated that he has confidence in Ms. Welborn, that the proposal was that she be paid by the hour ($100.00) without an accounting subject to review of the first accounting, that the consent was signed on that basis, that he will need to speak with her about this and suggest a one year accounting rather than two years. Mr. Cooper also suggested putting the matter over so that he can get a new consent and a new order. The Petition for Settlement of Seventh Account, etc. is continued to August 21, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: M. Armon Cooper for Petitioner/Trustee, with Miranda Gin, Trust Administrator from Zions Trust
The Court stated he had the following issued to be addressed: the payments to IBAR which combined with the Trustee's fee exceeds 1.9%, the outsourcing of trust administration in this small estate, and investments in ETFs and small trades without prior court approval as required by Local Rule 14.86J7. The Court was concerned that IBAR sought to justify its fees by contending it was very careful in overseeing expenditure of trust funds, yet it had reimbursed the beneficiary for travel expenses for trips to Mexico without obtaining any receipts. The Court was also unclear on the relationships, if any, between IBAR and its vendors. Mr. Cooper stated that he was unaware of any relationship between IBAR and its vendors. He stated that the beneficiary makes an annual trip to visit family in Mexico, and that the reimbursed travel expenses reflected mileage at $.25 per mile for two round trips from San Francisco to Mexico City, plus meals and lodging for four people for four nights at $100 per night. IBAR has repeatedly requested receipts from the beneficiary but the beneficiary has failed to provide them. IBAR has now informed the beneficiary that no further travel expenses will be advanced or reimbursed without receipts. With regard to the Trustee's fee, Mr. Cooper stated that the Trustee had only requested the minimum fee, but that if the minimum fee was waived it would bring the requested fees for trust administration to 1.5%. Ms. Gin stated her agreement to waive the trustee's minimum fee. Mr. Cooper stated that he was unaware local rule now required prior court approval for investing in EFTs. The Court conceded that the rule had only gone into effect in July of 2015, but it is now the policy of the Court to require prior approval for such investments. The Court stated that ETFs have been approved upon application, and that the Petitioner could chose between amending the petition to seek approval of the ETFs, or having the Court rule upon this accounting as presented and to set a status hearing regarding the investing in ETFs. Mr. Cooper requested that the Court adopt the latter course of action. The Petition for Approval of Accounting #6 was taken under submission. The Court set a Status hearing re ETF investments for October 14, 2015, at 9:00 a.m., in Department 204. The Trustee is to submit a report on the appropriateness of the ETF investments or file an application to approve them by that date.
Judge Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
